claims_verified.claim: Reuses the b82394a O_NOFOLLOW mechanism rather than reinventing it
claims_verified.evidence: report_path.py:69,120 use os.O_NOFOLLOW; queue.py:156,178 do the same; b82394a commit body confirms the mechanism

claims_verified.claim: .md suffix is enforced and rejects bare '.md'
claims_verified.evidence: report_path.py:97 (suffix != '.md' or stem == ''); test_report_path.py:22-29 parametrizes report.txt/config.toml/report/.md and asserts not target.exists()

claims_verified.claim: Planted symlink at target is refused with and without --force, decoy untouched
claims_verified.evidence: O_CREAT|O_EXCL|O_NOFOLLOW -> EEXIST/lstat (report_path.py:120-130) and _open_existing_for_force ELOOP (report_path.py:69,48-52); test_report_path.py:32-55 assert decoy content unchanged and symlink still present

claims_verified.claim: Confinement rejects ../, absolute-elsewhere, and parent-symlink escapes; accepts inside-root
claims_verified.evidence: report_path.py:106-111 realpath(parent).is_relative_to(realpath(root)); test_report_path.py:58-96 cover all four cases

claims_verified.claim: No-clobber: existing regular refused without --force, overwritten with; non-regular refused regardless
claims_verified.evidence: report_path.py:114-131, _open_existing_for_force fstat check at 74; test_report_path.py:99-148 (regular, dir, fifo, fifo-with-reader)
cli.py:109-110 report.error(...) -> exit 2; test_cli_report_out.py:51-56 asserts code==2 and file not created

claims_verified.claim: Markdown-safe output: heading + hard line breaks; stdout unchanged
claims_verified.evidence: report_path.py:27-39 renders '# loop-monitor report' + two-space hard breaks; cli.py:124-129 stdout path never calls render_markdown; test_report_path.py:158-166 and test_cli_report_out.py:41-48

test_assessment: Every acceptance criterion has a covering test. AC1: test_happy_path_writes_file, test_out_writes_file_and_prints_nothing, test_no_out_stdout_unchanged. AC2: test_non_md_suffix_is_refused (4 params) + test_out_refusal_exits_one. AC3: test_planted_symlink_is_refused_without/with_force (decoy content asserted). AC4: dotdot/absolute/parent-symlink/inside-root tests. AC5: existing-regular without/with force, directory, fifo, fifo-with-reader. AC6: render_markdown heading+separation test. AC7: full suite green (41 passed). Each new function has a failure-mode test: render_markdown (blank/non-blank lines), _refuse (ELOOP via symlink; EISDIR/ENXIO via dir/fifo), _open_existing_for_force (fifo-with-reader exercises the fstat-on-fd refusal without truncation, plus symlink ELOOP and dir EISDIR under force), write_report (missing-parent clean refusal). Confinement wiring for explicit --out-root is covered at the unit level (test_path_inside_explicit_out_root_is_accepted).
residual_risks: Parent-directory confinement uses realpath(parent) then opens with O_NOFOLLOW on the final component only; a live attacker swapping a parent component to an outside-root symlink between the realpath and the open could in theory redirect. This matches the accepted queue.py/b82394a posture and the stated threat model (sibling worktrees, not a racing adversary), so it is out of scope here.
No CLI-level test passes an explicit --out-root through main(); the flag's wiring (cli.py:114) is exercised only indirectly, though the guard behavior for explicit roots is unit-tested.
